title: Lophiolepis ellenbergii
intro: Lophiolepis ellenbergii, also known as the Indian spiny cedar or Himalayan cedar, is a small, evergreen tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Lophiolepis ellenbergii include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as in modern pharmaceutical formulations to leverage its health benefits.
